message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support (editing _termui_impl.py and termui.py). Please propose your files if different.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class - touches editor launch code in both files

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: We both need to touch the same two files (Editor class + edit()/editor() signatures) since both features add a new param there. True file-level split isn't possible. Proposal: you go first since you proposed first - implement your feature fully and send DONE. I will wait, then read the resulting file state and layer my process_priority param on top (as an additional trailing kwarg), taking care not to disturb your changes. I will send my own DONE after. Please confirm ACCEPT so I know to wait.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both genuinely need to touch both files, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Propose function-level split instead: I (agent1) will only touch edit()'s filename param handling + Editor.edit_file->edit_files (multi-file support, subprocess arg list). You (agent2) touch Editor.__init__/launch process_priority logic + edit()'s process_priority param. We each add our own param independently to the edit()/Editor signatures without removing the other's. I'll implement first and send DONE with exact diff summary so you can layer process_priority on top without clobbering my filename changes. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I will only add process_priority to Editor.__init__/launch logic and edit()'s process_priority param, leaving your filename/edit_files changes untouched. Waiting for your DONE with diff summary before I start editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirmed - I'll implement multi-file edit support now (edit()/edit_file->edit_files), and send DONE when finished with a summary of exact signature changes so you can layer process_priority on top cleanly.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file edit support. Summary of changes so you can layer process_priority cleanly: (1) In _termui_impl.py, Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) was renamed to Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) - it now joins quoted filenames with spaces before building the subprocess command string '{editor} {quoted_filenames}'. Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files([name]) instead of self.edit_file(name). (2) In termui.py, edit()'s filename param type is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None'; two @t.overload signatures were added above the implementation (one for text-only usage returning AnyStr|None with filename: None, one for filename usage - str or Iterable[str], keyword-only - returning None). The implementation does 'if isinstance(filename, str): filenames = (filename,) else: filenames = filename' then calls ed.edit_files(filenames). When adding process_priority, please add it as a new keyword param to Editor.__init__ and to both edit() overloads/impl signature (after 'extension', before 'filename' is probably safest, or trailing - your call), and thread it into the subprocess.Popen call in edit_files via e.g. subprocess.Popen(..., creationflags=... or preexec_fn=...) as appropriate for your OS mechanism. My patch is already written to patch.txt in my own workspace copy so there's no file clobbering risk on my end.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. Editor.__init__ gained process_priority: str|None=None param; edit_file() now builds popen_kwargs (creationflags on Windows via _PRIORITY_TO_WIN32_CREATIONFLAG, preexec_fn os.nice() on POSIX via _PRIORITY_TO_NICE_VALUE), with fallback to plain Popen call if the priority-augmented Popen raises OSError. Invalid/unmapped priority strings are silently ignored (dict.get returns None -> no kwargs added). termui.edit() gained a trailing process_priority: str|None=None param threaded into the Editor(...) constructor call, plus a docstring entry. I did not touch your filename/edit_files multi-file logic. Writing patch.txt now.
